phrases.summary: The text states that Elioud gave birth to (begot) Eleazar, establishing a father-son relationship in the genealogy.
The text states that Eleazar gave birth to (begot) Matthan, continuing the genealogical lineage through the next generation.
The text states that Matthan gave birth to (begot) Jacob, completing this sequence of the genealogy by naming the next generation.

phrases.phrase: Ἐλιοὺδ δὲ ἐγέννησεν τὸν Ἐλεάζαρ, Ἐλεάζαρ δὲ ἐγέννησεν τὸν Ματθάν, Ματθὰν δὲ ἐγέννησεν τὸν Ἰακώβ,
phrases.translation: Elioud begot Eleazar; Eleazar begot Matthan; Matthan begot Jacob.
phrases.summary: The text provides a short genealogical sequence, asserting successive birth relationships from Elioud to Eleazar, from Eleazar to Matthan, and from Matthan to Jacob.
